title: Seal Pups Spotted
summary:
The day we spent driving up the coast to Gualala was one of my favorites. We started the morning with some coffees from the store and went in search of a beach. We pulled into Black Point Beach and were surprised to find we were the only ones there. Compared to the beaches in Southern California, the beaches here are more frequent and less populated (at least this time of year). The beach was sandy and deserted so Maya got to zoom to her hearts content. After strolling the length of the beach, we walked out to a nearby bluff that was covered in bright pink flowers. I couldn't stop exclaiming and taking pictures. Our next stop just up the road was Tide Pool Beach where we hoped to see some sea creatures. Upon arriving, we were met by a Seal Docent who said the beach was closed but that we could see seal pups just up the way. We spent the next half an hour peering through the binoculars at some freshly born seals and protective mothers flopping about on the rocks. I could've watched them all day. Our last stop before making our way to our campsite was the Gualala Seafood Shack, where we got a cup of clam chowder (just okay) and a big fried prawn sandwich (very good!).

The campground was set along the Gualala River, about a mile from the ocean, in a big redwood forest. The afternoon we arrived was cloudy, so we spent the afternoon huddled up reading. The next morning was chilly but promised sun so we set out to the beach where I wanted to spend the day reading and searching for whales. Apparently this is a good place and good time of year to see gray whales and babies but so far no luck! It turned out the beach was extremely windy so the outing was short-lived, but we got a nice hot shower and spent another afternoon reading so I can't complain.
